evidence: Docstring states 'For finite G this is equivalent to Menger's theorem' — the Erdős–Menger conjecture is the infinite extension of Menger's theorem.
The problem statement requires A and B to be 'disjoint independent sets of vertices' (G.IsIndepSet A/B).
Docstring titles the variant 'Menger's theorem for infinite graphs (Aharoni–Berger [AhBe09])'.
Statement is about infinitely many n with d_n < d_{n+1} < d_{n+2} 'where d denotes the prime gap function'.
Variant asserts arbitrarily long increasing runs of the prime gap function d_n, proved via Maynard–Tao bounded gaps between primes.
Variant asserts arbitrarily long decreasing runs of the prime gap function d_n [BFT15].
e(n,r) counts edges forcing an edge contained in at least r triangles, in graphs where every edge lies in a triangle (cliqueFinset 3).
Part (ii) asks whether e(n,r+1)/e(n,r) -> 1 for the same triangle-count threshold function e(n,r).
Docstring: 'Ruzsa and Szemerédi [RuSz78] proved that e(n,r)=o(n^2) for any fixed r', citing the triple-systems paper behind the Ruzsa–Szemerédi problem.
The problem asks whether every qualifying almost-disjoint family 'has Property B' (HasPropertyB); the erdosproblems.com row is tagged 'Property B'.
Setup: 'An almost-disjoint family is one in which pairwise intersections are finite'; the hypothesis is (A i ∩ A j).Finite for i ≠ j.
Docstring cites Bernstein's Lemma: 'every countable system of infinite sets has Property B'.
File header names the problem 'Erdős Problem 61 -- Erdős–Hajnal Conjecture' and the decl states exactly that conjecture: for every H some c(H)>0 with f(n)=n^c.
The conjecture quantifies over graphs G 'that do not contain H as an induced subgraph' (¬∃ g : α ↪ Fin n, H = G.comap g).
The conclusion demands 'a clique or independent set of size ≥ f(n)' (G.cliqueNum ≥ f n).
The conclusion demands 'a clique or independent set of size ≥ f(n)' (G.indepNum ≥ f n).
The problem asks whether G 'must be the union of a bipartite graph and a graph with maximum degree less than n' (B.IsBipartite).
Header: 'A Ramsey–Turán problem of Erdős, Hajnal, Simonovits, Sós, and Szemerédi', asking whether rt(n; 4, n/log n) < (1/8 - c)n^2.
The statement asks for graphs containing 'either a K_4 or an independent set' (¬ G.CliqueFree 4).
The alternative conclusion is 'an independent set on at least n/log n vertices' (G.indepNum).
Docstring states the Fox–Loh–Zhao bound 'rt(n; 4, ne^{-f(n)}) ≥ (1/8 - o(1))n^2' from 'The critical window for the classical Ramsey-Turán problem'.
Docstring states Sudakov's complementary bound 'rt(n; 4, ne^{-f(n)}) = o(n^2)' from 'A few remarks on Ramsey-Turán-type problems'.
The Erdős–Gyárfás conjecture colours 'the edges of K_{r^2+1}' and looks at the induced K_{r+1} on r+1 vertices.
The hypothesis is an r-colouring of the edges (coloring : Sym2 V → Fin r), per [ErGy99] 'Split and balanced colorings of complete graphs'.
h_r(G) adds edges to a triangle-free G 'while preserving the property of being triangle-free' (H.CliqueFree 3), per [EGR98] 'How to decrease the diameter of triangle-free graphs'.
minNewEdges r G is the least number of added edges 'so that it has diameter at most r' (H.ediam ≤ r).
